briefs.title: Assisted Living Rental Units Removed from BC Residential Tenancy Act Protection
briefs.summary: As of February 23, 2026, rental units inside assisted living residences (housing that provides personal care services to residents) are no longer covered by BC's Residential Tenancy Act. This means the standard tenant protections — such as rules on rent increases, eviction notices, and security deposits — no longer apply to those units.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or own rental units inside an assisted living residence, stop applying Residential Tenancy Act rules to new and existing tenancies immediately.

key_dates.label: BC assisted living rental units exemption from Residential Tenancy Act takes effect
